Overall:
The following processes are considered effective for the removal of diuron: chlorine (up to 100 percent), hydrogen peroxide (up to 90 percent), membrane filtration (up to 86 percent), membrane separation (up to 92 percent), ozone (70 to 100 percent), powdered activated carbon (75 to 100 percent), ultraviolet irradiation (up to 99 percent), and ultraviolet irradiation hydrogen peroxide (up to 100 percent). Performance of these technologies can vary based on water quality and treatment characteristics.
Conventional treatment was not effective by itself at 30 removal.
